question: Consider v = [0,1,1,0,1,0], an individual from the binary vector space. The associated fitness function computes the sum of the bits in the vector. Select the correct statements: 1) v has 7 neighbors at Hamming distance 1; 2) there is at least one neighbor at Hamming distance 1 with a higher fitness than v; 3) there is at least one neighbor at Hamming distance 1 with a lower fitness than v; 4) there is no neighbor at Hamming distance 1 with the same fitness as v; 5) there is at least one neighbor at Hamming distance 1 with the same fitness as v.

Consider M, a genetic mutation operator defined for the permutation representation of size n. Select the correct statements: 1) M is a unary operator; 2) M always randomly selects 2 genes to apply the mutation; 3) the result of M may have a different size than n; 4) the result of M is a permutation.
Consider the problem minimize f over a genotype solution space where the variables are encoded as binary choices. The genotype solution space for this problem is:

answers.text: the set of permutations of size n
the set of binary vectors of size n

In an evolutionary strategy class algorithm, the recombination operation: 1) in average, selects half of the individuals for recombination; 2) is used only in problems with constraints; 3) uses the current population; 4) happens only once during the algorithm; 5) has a low probability; 6) creates a descendants multiset that is usually much larger than the current population; 7) happens only once, after the first population is generated; 8) is universal; 9) influences the chromosome structure.

question: In an evolutionary algorithm, the fitness function: 1) evaluates the algorithm quality; 2) depends on the problem to be solved; 3) evaluates the speed of finding the solution versus resource consumption; 4) must be modified at each iteration; 5) selects the individuals that will reproduce; 6) selects the individuals that will pass into the next generation; 7) evaluates the quality of the current generation versus the initial generation; 8) evaluates the quality of the current generation versus the previous generation; 9) evaluates the quality of each candidate.
